spec_section_updates.section_4: pretool-bash-safety.sh line 805: `check_and_consume_allowlist "$COMMAND" && exit 0` present inside `if ! check_systemctl_targets_all_dev` block, before `exit 2` at line 809. pretool-git-privilege-guard.py line 583: `if _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return` present in `_evaluate_push()`, after forbidden-ref-mutation check (line 576) and before CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE env check (line 586). Call site at line 666 updated to `_evaluate_push(command, data)`. Python syntax: clean (py_compile exit 0).

qa.overall_assessment: Both M1 and M2 changes are correct and in place. All six acceptance criteria verified against the actual code. Python syntax is valid.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: check_and_consume_allowlist appears inside the if ! check_systemctl_targets_all_dev block, BEFORE its exit 2
success_criteria_results.criterion: check_and_consume_allowlist is at line 805 inside the inner block, before exit 2 at line 809
success_criteria_results.details: Line 805: `check_and_consume_allowlist "$COMMAND" && exit 0` — inside `if ! check_systemctl_targets_all_dev` block (line 804), before `exit 2` at line 809.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: non-happy systemctl block still exits 2 without /allow grant
success_criteria_results.criterion: The new call is INSIDE the if ! check_systemctl_targets_all_dev condition; without a grant the block still exits 2
success_criteria_results.details: check_and_consume_allowlist returns non-zero when no grant is active; execution continues to exit 2 at line 809. The outer `if ! check_systemctl_targets_all_dev` condition still gates entry to this entire block.
success_criteria_results.evidence: pretool-bash-safety.sh:802-811 — outer condition at line 804, allowlist call at 805, exit 2 at 809

success_criteria_results.details: Line 583: `if _check_git_allowlist(command, data): return` — placed before `_block_default_deny_push` call at line 587.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: git push still blocked without /allow grant
success_criteria_results.criterion: Code path reaches _block_default_deny_push when allowlist returns False
success_criteria_results.details: Without a grant, _check_git_allowlist returns False, `if` at line 583 is not taken, execution falls through to CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE check at line 586 and then _block_default_deny_push at line 587.
Line 576: `if _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation(command)` — line 583: `if _check_git_allowlist(command, data)`. Correct order: forbidden-ref-mutation check precedes allowlist check.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Call order in _evaluate_push() is: (1) _inline_env_present, (2) _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation, (3) _check_git_allowlist, (4) CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE env check
success_criteria_results.criterion: Exact call order in _evaluate_push matches specification
success_criteria_results.details: Line 574: _inline_env_present check. Line 576: _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation check. Line 583: _check_git_allowlist call. Line 586: CLAUDE_PUSH_COMMAND_ACTIVE env check. All four in correct order.

qa._success_criteria_rule: 6 AC items in spec, 6 entries produced.

root_cause_verification.rationale: M1: check_and_consume_allowlist inserted at line 805 inside non-happy systemctl block, matching the pattern of the five git-rule blocks (lines 984, 995, 1007, 1027, 1110). M2: _check_git_allowlist added to _evaluate_push() at line 583, function signature refactored to accept data (matching _evaluate_reset_hard, _evaluate_direct_ref_mutation, _evaluate_forbidden_plumbing pattern), call site at line 666 updated.

build_verification.skip_reason: Hook files, not a deployed web service. Python syntax validated. Bash syntax not separately checked but hook is syntactically simple (single line insertion).

regression_test_results.details: grep confirms single call site at line 666: _evaluate_push(command, data) — signature and caller are consistent
_push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation at line 576 precedes _check_git_allowlist at line 583; force-push blocked before allowlist is consulted

e2e_enforcement.blocking_reason: BA spec explicitly states: non-UI hook/config change; no rendered UI changes, no browser interaction, no Playwright invocation. Both modified files are PreToolUse hooks executed by the Claude Code harness, not user-visible rendering paths.
